questions.prompt: An expansionary monetary policy (the Fed lowering its FFR target) shifts which curve in which direction in the short-run AD-AS model?
questions.explanation: Lower rates raise interest-sensitive spending, shifting aggregate demand right. SRAS doesn't move from a rate change (it shifts on cost or expectation shocks).

questions.prompt: Which Fed tool sets a floor on short-term rates in the post-QE era?
questions.explanation: Because abundant reserves persist, paying IORB lets the Fed lift short rates without draining reserves. Open-market ops still occur but aren't the primary floor mechanism.

questions.prompt: Which of the following were tools the Fed used at the zero lower bound in 2008-2014?
questions.explanation: The Fed used QE, forward guidance, and emergency facilities. It did NOT use negative IORB; rates stayed at or above zero throughout.

questions.prompt: Imagine inflation is well above the Fed's 2% target and unemployment is right at the natural rate. Which way would you expect the Fed to move its policy rate, and what would happen to the AD curve in the short run?
questions.explanation: With inflation above target and no slack to protect, the Fed tightens. Higher rates discourage interest-sensitive spending, shifting AD left and pulling inflation back toward target.

furtherReading.title: Inflation Decline Continues to Support a Soft Landing Along the Nonlinear Phillips Curve
furtherReading.why: SF Fed analysis of the 2022-24 disinflation showing how a contraction in aggregate demand from Fed tightening brought inflation down without a recession — a textbook AD-AS soft landing.
